pullquote_provenance.label: Tom Price-Daniel · Co-founder, Teamed
pullquote_provenance.rule_compliance: Per pullquote provenance rule — Tom-bylined pullquotes drafted in-voice. No <cite> source line required. Brand-voice exempt per linter PULLQUOTE_BRAND_VOICE.

high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty: prompt-supplied 'only Reilly v. Alcan Aluminum carve-outs' framing verified — Reilly v. Alcan Aluminum Corp., 272 Ga. 279 (2000) is the Georgia Supreme Court reaffirmation of the rejection of the public-policy tort. Borden v. Johnson, 196 Ga. App. 288 (1990) is the earlier Court of Appeals authority. Page cites both, with framing that the public-policy DOOR is closed (which is accurate) rather than 'Reilly carved out exceptions' (which would invert the holding).

factual_uncertainty: prompt-supplied 'NO implied-contract from handbooks' verified — Fletcher v. Amax, Inc., 160 Ga. App. 692 (1981) is the leading authority; Ellison v. DeKalb County (1999) confirms. Page reflects accurately, noting that Georgia does NOT require a disclaimer because the underlying claim does not exist (unlike Alabama under Hoffman-La Roche, which DOES require disclaimer).

factual_uncertainty: prompt-supplied 'NO covenant of good faith' verified — Ga. Power Co. v. Busbin, 242 Ga. 612 (1978) is the Georgia Supreme Court authority rejecting the implied covenant of good faith in at-will employment. Page reflects accurately.

factual_uncertainty: final-pay framing verified — Georgia has no state final-paycheck statute. FLSA default (next regular payday) controls. Confirmed via Georgia DOL public guidance and absence of any final-pay provision in OCGA Title 34 Chapter 7.

factual_uncertainty: Georgia is a 180-day non-deferral state for EEOC purposes — verified. Georgia Commission on Equal Opportunity is limited to state-employee discrimination (no private-employer EEOC work-sharing agreement covering Title VII charges), so the 300-day deferral extension does not apply.

factual_uncertainty: Form DOL-800 separation notice — verified as the Georgia DOL Separation Notice required under OCGA § 34-8-190 and DOL rules. Required for each separated employee at separation regardless of cause or headcount.
factual_uncertainty: Bostock v. Clayton County originated as a Georgia case — verified. Clayton County is in Georgia. The aside is accurate.
factual_uncertainty: Eleventh Circuit appellate jurisdiction over Georgia federal courts — verified. The Eleventh Circuit covers Alabama, Florida, and Georgia.
factual_uncertainty: No Georgia mini-WARN — verified. Georgia has no state mini-WARN statute. Federal WARN Act is the entire mass-layoff framework.
